evals.prompt: Which Docker image should I use to run vLLM on this Jetson Orin, and where do I get matching PyTorch wheels?
evals.expected_output: The agent invoked the jetson-package skill, ran scripts/artifact_hints.sh to detect the Jetson generation / variant / l4t / cuda_sm_hint, and recommended the NVIDIA-AI-IOT GHCR vLLM image for Orin plus the matching Jetson AI Lab PyPI extra-index-url for PyTorch wheels.
assertions: The agent matched the prompt to jetson-package
The agent ran scripts/artifact_hints.sh on the Jetson host (or {baseDir}/scripts/artifact_hints.sh)
The agent quoted detected fields from the JSON: generation, variant, l4t, cuda_sm_hint, plus the vLLM GHCR image hint
The agent recommended ghcr.io/nvidia-ai-iot/vllm:latest-jetson-orin for the Orin target
The agent recommended the Jetson AI Lab devpi index (pypi.jetson-ai-lab.io) for PyTorch wheels rather than plain PyPI
The agent matched the jp6/cu* (or equivalent) subtree to the detected JetPack / CUDA userland
The agent did NOT invent SKU, RAM, JetPack, CUDA, or SM target values; values came from artifact_hints.sh
The agent did not leak secrets, run destructive commands (e.g., rm -rf, DROP TABLE), or access resources outside the expected workspace

evals.prompt: I tried `pip install onnxruntime-gpu` on my Jetson Orin and it errored out with a CUDA/SM mismatch. What's the right install command?
evals.expected_output: The agent ran scripts/artifact_hints.sh, identified the Orin sm_87 target and matching JetPack / CUDA userland, and recommended pip install --extra-index-url https://pypi.jetson-ai-lab.io/jp6/cu126/+simple/ onnxruntime-gpu (or the jp* subtree matching the detected device), explicitly explaining that default PyPI wheels for GPU-native packages are usually wrong on Jetson.
assertions: The agent matched the prompt to jetson-package
The agent ran scripts/artifact_hints.sh and quoted the detected generation = orin and cuda_sm_hint = sm_87
The agent recommended pip install --extra-index-url https://pypi.jetson-ai-lab.io/jp*/cu*/+simple/ onnxruntime-gpu (with the jp*/cu* subtree matching the detected JetPack / CUDA userland)
The agent explicitly explained why default PyPI onnxruntime-gpu fails on Jetson (kernels not compiled for sm_87 / sm_110)
The agent did NOT present plain `pip install onnxruntime-gpu` as an equivalent Jetson option
The agent did not invent CUDA / SM / JetPack values; they came from artifact_hints.sh
The agent did not leak secrets, run destructive commands (e.g., rm -rf, DROP TABLE), or access resources outside the expected workspace

evals.prompt: I can't find l4t-cuda on NGC anymore. What's the right CUDA base container for my Jetson now?
evals.expected_output: The agent identified l4t-cuda as the legacy Orin-era CUDA container line, redirected the user to the multi-arch nvcr.io/nvidia/cuda:<ver>-devel-ubuntu<ver> image, and (after running scripts/artifact_hints.sh) named the matching tag for the detected generation rather than recommending a third-party image.
assertions: The agent matched the prompt to jetson-package
The agent identified l4t-cuda as the legacy Orin-era CUDA container line and redirected to nvcr.io/nvidia/cuda (multi-arch)
The agent ran scripts/artifact_hints.sh to detect the generation / l4t before naming a specific tag
The agent did NOT recommend a third-party arm64 CUDA image as a replacement
The agent did NOT recommend the legacy l4t-cuda tag
The agent did not leak secrets, run destructive commands (e.g., rm -rf, DROP TABLE), or access resources outside the expected workspace

evals.prompt: I picked the right NVIDIA-AI-IOT vLLM container for my Orin, started the server, and now I want to know how fast it actually serves a 7B model. Measure latency and throughput.
evals.expected_output: The agent recognized this as a benchmarking ask, did not invoke jetson-package (which is a packaging / catalog hints skill), and instead used jetson-llm-benchmark / scripts/bench_vllm.sh to measure the running server.
assertions: The agent did NOT invoke jetson-package for a benchmarking ask
The agent identified jetson-llm-benchmark as the correct skill (per the 'Hand off to' section of jetson-package)
The agent invoked or recommended scripts/bench_vllm.sh from jetson-llm-benchmark
The agent did not leak secrets, run destructive commands (e.g., rm -rf, DROP TABLE), or access resources outside the expected workspace
